title: cordia grandis
intro: Cordia grandis, also known as the wild plum or white codoneaster, is a deciduous tree native to India and Southeast Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cordia grandis include its potential to reduce fever, treat respiratory infections, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Cordia grandis are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as dried powder and oil extracts.
